items.title: Polynomial degree too low on curved data
items.scenario: Predict y = x² + noise with degree-1 polynomial regression. Training MSE stays high at 2.1; validation MSE is 2.3 on 5,000 points. Increasing data to 50,000 barely changes either metric.
items.explanation: A line cannot bend — classic underfitting. Degree-2 polynomial drops both errors sharply; the problem was capacity, not sample size.

items.title: Deep decision tree on a small tabular set
items.scenario: Churn prediction with 400 rows and a decision tree at max_depth=20. Training accuracy hits 100%; validation accuracy is 62%, far below a depth-4 tree at 78% val accuracy.
items.explanation: The deep tree memorizes training quirks — overfitting (high variance). Shallower tree trades some training fit for better validation generalization.

items.title: Neural net too small for image task
items.scenario: MNIST with one hidden layer of 4 units: train accuracy stalls at 82%, validation at 81%. A CNN with two conv layers reaches 99% on both.
items.explanation: Tiny network underfits — insufficient capacity (high bias). Scaling architecture reduces underfitting until variance becomes the limiter.

items.title: Heavy dropout on an already weak model
items.scenario: Text classifier with a small embedding + linear head; 50% dropout and strong L2. Train F1 is 0.45, validation F1 is 0.43 on 100k labeled tweets.
items.explanation: Regularization on an under-capacity model worsens bias. Loosening dropout and adding a hidden layer raises train F1 first; then tune regularization if val lags.

items.title: Training too long without early stopping
items.scenario: Fine-tuning a language model: training loss keeps decreasing for 20 epochs while validation perplexity improves until epoch 6, then rises steadily through epoch 20.
items.explanation: Continued training after the validation minimum fits training-specific noise — overfitting. Early stopping at epoch 6 would have frozen the best generalizing weights.
